## overview

Recover and properly implement the use-case pages revamp that was planned in a prior chat but never landed. The work rewrites the 1632-line marketing shell into an activation-focused layout, migrates CRM/Compliance bespoke pages to thin wrappers, wires skill bundles into the UI, and adds a "Use cases" category to the /docs index and sidebar navigation.

# Use-Case Pages Recovery Plan

## UX Benefits

This revamp delivers five concrete improvements to the docs site experience:

- **Discoverability from /docs**: Today, the 17 use-case landing pages are invisible from the documentation hub and sidebar. Users must already know the URL (e.g. `/compliance`) or find them buried in the verticals index. Adding a "Use cases" category to both the `/docs` index grid and the sidebar navigation means every visitor to `/docs` sees the full catalog of domain-specific capabilities alongside getting-started guides and reference docs.

- **Activation-first layout**: The current shell is a 1632-line marketing scroller with seven full-bleed sections (problem, scenarios, outcomes, how-it-works, capabilities, architecture, case study). A visitor who already understands the product must scroll through all of this to find a CLI install command or sandbox link. The rewritten shell leads with "Activate this use case" (CLI install, sandbox access, Stage 0.5 detection) so a returning user can act in seconds.

- **Skill bundle visibility**: Neotoma ships per-vertical agent skills (e.g. `log-decision` for agent-auth), and `vertical_skills.generated.ts` already contains structured data for them, but nothing in the UI renders it. Surfacing skill bundles on each use-case page shows users the exact agent behaviors they get when they install a pack, bridging the gap between "what does this vertical do?" and "what will my agent actually do differently?"

- **Consistency across all use cases**: CRM and Compliance are currently 1400-line bespoke pages with their own layouts, copy structures, and CTAs that diverge from the 15 pages using `UseCaseLandingShell`. Migrating them to thin config wrappers means every use-case page shares the same information architecture, reducing cognitive load when comparing verticals.

- **Reduced dead-end paths**: Today, vertical landing pages link to `/install` and `/docs` in the CTA but have no sidebar or breadcrumb context. Integrating them into the docs sidebar gives users a persistent navigation frame, eliminating the "where am I?" disorientation that full-bleed marketing pages create inside a documentation context.

---

## Current State (Forensics Summary)

What exists in the repo now:

- [`frontend/src/components/subpages/use_case_landing/UseCaseLandingShell.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/use_case_landing/UseCaseLandingShell.tsx) -- 1632-line marketing shell with `UseCaseConfig` type (renamed from `VerticalLandingShell` / `VerticalConfig` since the prior chat)
- [`frontend/src/data/verticals.ts`](frontend/src/data/verticals.ts) -- `VERTICALS_DATA` array with slug, title, tagline, marketingHref for all verticals
- [`frontend/src/data/sandbox_packs.ts`](frontend/src/data/sandbox_packs.ts) -- `SANDBOX_PACKS` array identifying which verticals have sandbox packs
- [`frontend/src/data/vertical_skills.generated.ts`](frontend/src/data/vertical_skills.generated.ts) -- orphaned generated file with `VERTICAL_SKILLS_BY_SLUG` data (gitignored, no generator script, no consumers)
- [`frontend/src/site/site_data.ts`](frontend/src/site/site_data.ts) -- `USE_CASE_LANDING_PATHS` (line 336), `DOC_NAV_CATEGORIES` (line 356) -- no "Use cases" category
- [`frontend/src/components/subpages/DocsIndexPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/DocsIndexPage.tsx) -- `DOC_CATEGORIES` (line 32) -- no "Use cases" category; vestigial `categoryUseCases` i18n branch at line 300
- [`frontend/src/i18n/dictionaries.ts`](frontend/src/i18n/dictionaries.ts) -- `categoryUseCases` key exists in all locales (ready for use)
- 15 landing pages use `UseCaseLandingShell` via config pattern (e.g. `AgentAuthLandingPage.tsx`)
- [`CrmLandingPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/CrmLandingPage.tsx) (1397 lines) and [`ComplianceLandingPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/ComplianceLandingPage.tsx) (1405 lines) are bespoke, not using the shell

What is missing:

- `scripts/generate_frontend_vertical_skills.ts` -- generator for the skills data file
- `packages/_skill_templates/` -- source directory the generator reads
- `frontend/src/components/VerticalSkillBundles.tsx` -- component to render skills on each page
- `package.json` `generate:vertical-skills` script
- Shell rewrite (activation-focused layout, ~580 lines replacing 1632)
- CRM/Compliance thin-wrapper migration
- "Use cases" category in `DOC_CATEGORIES` and `DOC_NAV_CATEGORIES`
- Tests (`vertical_skills_generated.test.ts`, `docs_use_cases_navigation.test.ts`)
- v0.8.0 release supplement update

---

## Implementation

### 1. Skill bundles pipeline

The generated file `vertical_skills.generated.ts` exists but its source (`packages/_skill_templates/`) does not. Two sub-steps:

**1a.** Create `packages/_skill_templates/<vertical>/<skill>.md` source files. The generated data references 5 verticals with skills (agent-auth, compliance, crm, financial-ops, personal-data). Create the Markdown templates with YAML front matter (`name`, `description`, `vertical`, `neotoma_pack`) matching the data already in the generated file. Verify harness copies exist at paths like `packages/claude-code-plugin/skills/<vertical>/<skill>/SKILL.md`.

**1b.** Create `scripts/generate_frontend_vertical_skills.ts`. It should:
- Read `packages/_skill_templates/<vertical>/<skill>.md`, parse YAML front matter
- Check which harness packages (`claude-code-plugin`, `cursor-hooks`, `codex-hooks`, `opencode-plugin`, `claude-agent-sdk-adapter`) host copies
- Write to `frontend/src/data/vertical_skills.generated.ts`
- Add `"generate:vertical-skills": "tsx scripts/generate_frontend_vertical_skills.ts"` to `package.json` scripts

### 2. VerticalSkillBundles component

Create `frontend/src/components/VerticalSkillBundles.tsx`:
- Import `getVerticalSkills` from the generated file
- Accept `verticalSlug` prop, render a card list of skills with name, description, pack, and harness links
- Render nothing when no skills exist for the vertical

### 3. Rewrite UseCaseLandingShell

Rewrite [`UseCaseLandingShell.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/use_case_landing/UseCaseLandingShell.tsx) from 1632 lines to ~580 lines with these sections:

- **Hero** (title, highlight, description, feature badges)
- **Scope at a glance** (record types count, skill bundles count, recommended tools count, sandbox availability)
- **Activate this use case** (cards: `neotoma packs install <slug>`, "Try in sandbox" conditional on `SANDBOX_PACKS`, "Let Neotoma detect it" for Stage 0.5)
- **Why this exists** (problem statement, callout)
- **Native record types** (entity examples from `VERTICALS_DATA`)
- **Skill bundles** (via `VerticalSkillBundles`, conditional)
- **In practice** (first scenario from config)
- **Reference and next steps** (links to /types, /schema-management, /agent-instructions, /cli)
- **CTA** (install + sandbox + browse all)

Make marketing-heavy fields on `UseCaseConfig` optional so the 15 existing shell-driven pages continue to compile without changes. The new layout ignores them; the old fields are not removed from the type.

### 4. CRM and Compliance migration

Replace [`CrmLandingPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/CrmLandingPage.tsx) (~1397 lines) and [`ComplianceLandingPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/ComplianceLandingPage.tsx) (~1405 lines) with ~70-line thin wrappers exporting a `UseCaseConfig` and rendering `<UseCaseLandingShell config={CONFIG} />`.

### 5. /docs integration

**5a.** Add "Use cases" category to `DOC_CATEGORIES` in [`DocsIndexPage.tsx`](frontend/src/components/subpages/DocsIndexPage.tsx) after "Integrations":

```typescript
{
  title: "Use cases",
  items: [
    { label: "All use cases", href: "/use-cases", desc: "Browse every vertical pack" },
    ...VERTICALS_DATA.map((v) => ({
      label: v.title,
      href: v.marketingHref,
      desc: v.tagline,
    })),
  ],
},
```

**5b.** Add "Use cases" category to `DOC_NAV_CATEGORIES` in [`site_data.ts`](frontend/src/site/site_data.ts) before "Record types":

```typescript
{
  title: "Use cases",
  sidebarTitle: "Use cases",
  items: [
    { label: "All use cases", href: "/use-cases", icon: "Layers" },
    ...VERTICALS_DATA.map((v) => ({
      label: v.title,
      href: v.marketingHref,
      icon: "Layers",
    })),
  ],
},
```

Import `VERTICALS_DATA` from `@/data/verticals` in both files.

### 6. Tests

- `tests/services/verticals/vertical_skills_generated.test.ts` -- asserts generated file structure matches skill template sources
- `tests/unit/docs_use_cases_navigation.test.ts` -- asserts "Use cases" group exists in `DOC_NAV_CATEGORIES`, includes `/use-cases` and every vertical from `VERTICALS_DATA`

### 7. Release notes

Add "Use case pages revamp" bullet to [`docs/releases/in_progress/v0.8.0/github_release_supplement.md`](docs/releases/in_progress/v0.8.0/github_release_supplement.md).
